reasoning: The base rate for a company that just raised $10B privately in January 2025 to file for an IPO within the next five months is extremely low. There are only about 40 days left until the resolution date (July 1, 2025), and there have been no credible recent rumors that Databricks has hired investment bankers or confidentially submitted an S-1, which are typical precursors that leak to the press. Over the past six months, the CEO has continuously emphasized that the company has no urgent need to go public, explicitly stating that a potential IPO could happen in the second half of 2025 or even in 2026 at the earliest. In the absence of any indications of active IPO preparation, the probability of an SEC Form S-1 filing or a formal announcement by July 1, 2025, is as close to zero as possible.
